title: SECURITY/IP: scrub proprietary reference-repo name from committed files + git history
description: SECURITY/IP: the proprietary, non-public reference-repo name (see bd memory 'the-python-uses-edge-reference-prototype-and-its'; name intentionally NOT repeated here) appears in committed files, violating the documented constraint that it must never be committed. KNOWN LOCATIONS: (1) docs/design-plans/2026-06-23-uses-edges.md — 5 occurrences, introduced in design-phase commits eaa6643 and 77772ad; (2) .beads/issues.jsonl — the committed passive export contains the name in pre-existing issue descriptions and possibly the stored memory. The Phase 3 differential-gate deliverables (tests/diff.test.ts, docs/uses-edges-differential.md, tests/diff-allowlist.json) are CLEAN/generic — verified by grep. REMEDIATION (owner decision required): (a) scrub working-tree occurrences in the design plan, replacing with generic 'reference repo'; (b) audit .beads/issues.jsonl + dolt-stored issues/memory and redact; (c) decide whether to rewrite git history (filter-repo) to purge from prior commits before any push to a shared/PUBLIC remote — DESTRUCTIVE, explicit decision needed. Do NOT push main to a public remote until resolved.
notes: SCRUB COMPLETE (local + git + remote + dolt + deciduous). Done: design-plan working tree; full git history rewrite on main + gh-pages via git-filter-repo (blobs + commit messages); force-pushed clean (main 98a23d2, gh-pages 6559cd6); beads dolt comments+events tables scrubbed via dolt CLI 2.1.9 and committed; deciduous.db scrubbed via SQLite+VACUUM (0 raw bytes); clean bd export committed+pushed. Verified 0 occurrences across all 7 surfaces. ONLY REMAINING (external, owner action): GitHub retains unreachable pre-rewrite commits accessible by SHA until GC + the name was publicly exposed for a real period — request GitHub Support to purge cached views/unreachable commits and review forks; treat the name as historically disclosed. Backups: scratchpad/orrery-prescrub-backup.bundle (git) + beads-prescrub.tar.gz (dolt).
close_reason: Scrub complete across all 7 surfaces (git history main+gh-pages, public remote, working tree, beads dolt, deciduous DB). Remaining external item (GitHub Support purge of unreachable pre-rewrite commits) WAIVED by owner: new repo, no users, acceptable residual risk.

title: Language plugins: top 10 + Elixir
description: Expand parsing breadth beyond Python/TS/TSX/JS to the ~top-10 languages + Elixir, out of the box. Target set (additive to existing): Java, C#, C/C++, Go, Rust, Ruby, PHP, Kotlin/Swift, + Elixir. Each plugin contributes structure+import+class AND its uses-extraction (per the pluggable capability established in orrery-2uv). tree-sitter grammars available via tree-sitter-wasms (mind ABI pin). Each language = grammar + queries + import resolver + uses-extractor.

title: Entry-point / public-API detection per language
description: Set the existing entry/public node fields (schema + renderer already support them; extractor doesn't populate). Python: __main__, pyproject [project.scripts], __all__. TS/JS: package.json bin, index barrels, exports. Reference: prototype extract.py.

title: uses edges: class-level intra-repo symbol usage
description: Port the prototype's class-level 'uses' edges (symbol usage between classes/functions) to tree-sitter. Build a symbol table from imports, then scan class bodies for references. Biggest remaining fidelity gain; higher effort. Reference: ORGaNICs/scratch/codemap/extract.py.
notes: Design complete and committed: docs/design-plans/2026-06-23-uses-edges.md (commit eaa6643). Architecture = Hybrid (optional per-plugin UsesCapability; orchestrator stays generic via two-pass engine). 5 implementation phases defined; ready for an implementation plan. Renderer granularity follow-up = orrery-d0a. Deciduous goal node 6, decisions 11-12, outcome 13.
close_reason: uses-edges implementation complete: 5 phases all APPROVED (commits 7b410eb..145fa96), 12 pass/1 skip/0 fail, tsc clean, final review 0 issues, test coverage PASS, human test plan committed. Follow-ups tracked: orrery-893, orrery-t1n.

comments.author: Kyle Stratis
comments.text: Design phase tracked in epic orrery-zhc

title: uses-edges: cache parsed trees across pass 1/pass 2 to avoid double-parse
description: Phase 2 code review (orrery-31p) flagged that pass 2 of the uses engine re-reads and re-parses every uses-capable source file that pass 1 already read/parsed (src/extract/index.ts pass 2 loop duplicates the pass 1 read/parse). Correct but O(2x) parse cost on languages with a uses capability. The two-pass split is intentional in the design; optimizing requires caching pass-1 parse results for reuse in pass 2. Out of scope for Phase 2 per reviewer; file as follow-up. Low priority — only matters for extraction perf on large repos.

title: Renderer: granularity views for uses edges (module / class / all + expand-module)
description: orrery-2uv emits class-level 'uses' edges that are class-preferred with module fallback, so a single scene commingles module-layer relations (import edges, module->module) and class-layer relations (uses edges, class->class and class->module). Users should be able to tell at a glance whether they're viewing module relations, class relations, or all. This is a RENDERER feature, enabled for free because every edge endpoint already carries its node 'kind' and the parent hierarchy is in the CodeMap. Possible UX: a granularity filter (module-layer / class-layer / all), and/or an expand-on-click interaction where a module 'dot' explodes to reveal its contained classes and their inter/intra-class uses edges (collapse back to a single module node by default). Builds on orrery-2uv; no extraction changes required.
acceptance_criteria: Renderer can filter the scene to module-layer-only, class-layer-only, or all; granularity is visually unambiguous at a glance; (stretch) module nodes expand to reveal contained classes and collapse back.
notes: Surfaced during orrery-2uv design (Phase 3). Decision: keep extraction faithful (module fallback), solve display clarity here. See deciduous goal node 6.

title: Thin agent skill wrapper for orrery CLI
description: Agent skill that detects language/root, runs the CLI, and for repos with dynamic wiring (registries/plugins/DI) generates an analyzer on the fly and feeds it via --augment. Per design: the agent writes dynamic hooks on demand; do NOT bake a dynamic hook into the CLI.

title: bun build --compile single binary
description: Embed assets so the compiled binary is self-contained: template.html, vendor JS, core tree-sitter.wasm, grammar wasms. See TODO comments in src/render/index.ts and src/extract/treesitter.ts. Mind the web-tree-sitter 0.22.6 ABI pin.

title: uses edges: function-level 'zoom in'
description: Extend 'uses' edges below class granularity: emit function nodes and function->symbol uses edges so users can 'zoom in' on intra-class wiring. Deferred from orrery-2uv (class-level uses edges). Low priority: for orienting in a new repo, class-level is the primary signal; function-level is a drill-down nicety. Requires adding function-node extraction (not currently emitted).
